data.title: AAII survey shows increase in bullish sentiment
data.description:
Bullish sentiment about the market increased a little, as investors continue to assess U.S. President Donald Trump’s tariff policy.

The American Association of Individual Investors’ (AAII) closely watched survey on the retail traders indicated that bearish sentiment decreased during the week, after three consecutive weeks of rises and touching a one-year-high of 47.3%. Bullish sentiment is still below the historical average of 37.5%.

A total of 29.2% of respondents, as per the AAII Sentiment survey, showed optimism this week, rising from last week’s figure of 28.4%. Meanwhile, bearish views about the stock market outlook decreased to 40.5% for the week ending Feb. 19, versus 47.3% the week before.

Trump on Tuesday said he would probably announce 25% tariffs on imports of cars, semiconductors and pharmaceuticals on April 2, marking a widening of targeted sectors after previously imposing levies on imports of steel and aluminum. Previously, he targeted Canada and Mexico with 25% tariffs and China with 10%.

While investors and analysts remain cautious about inflation and the ongoing trade war, Oppenheimer chief investment strategist John Stoltzfus said the firm remains "positive on equities" and is confident in the market's trajectory, even amid ongoing trade tensions and tariff uncertainties.

According to AAII, neutral sentiment, or expectations that stock prices will stay essentially unchanged over the next six months, stood at 30.3% for the week, versus 24.3% last week.

The weekly survey, which has been conducted by the AAII since 1987, asks respondents for their thoughts on where the market is heading in the next six months.

The S&P 500 Index (SP500) rose 0.5% in the last one week, while Dow Jones Industrial Average Index (DJI) recorded a marginal decline.

data.title: Big Banks: Seeking Alpha Analysts vs. Wall Street
data.description:
US banks kicked off the fourth-quarter earnings season in high gear. Five of the top six banks—Bank of America (NYSE:BAC), JPMorgan Chase (NYSE:JPM), Goldman Sachs (NYSE:GS), Citigroup (NYSE:C), and Morgan Stanley (NYSE:MS)—reported better-than-expected results.

Wells Fargo (NYSE:WFC) was the only major U.S. bank to post a miss on their revenue expectations, while fourth-quarter per-share earnings were higher than forecasts.

With the banking sector expected to benefit from a pivoting U.S. Federal Reserve and a pro-business U.S. administration, shares of the big banks have been on the march higher. It's no surprise, with many Seeking Alpha and Wall Street analysts bullish on this group of stocks.

As seen in the chart above, Wall Street has more Strong Buys on the sector, though many SA analysts are at Buy as well. Interestingly, the only Strong Sells are seen at Jamie Dimon-led JPMorgan Chase (NYSE:JPM), and while that represents a small minority of the Street, the majority of SA is rated Hold.

Meanwhile, Morgan Stanley (NYSE:MS) and Wells Fargo (NYSE:WFC) feature a wide spectrum of opinions, compared to the bullish tilt on Bank of America (NYSE:BAC), Goldman Sachs (NYSE:GS), and Citigroup (NYSE:C)

More on banks

data.title: HWH International announces 1-for-5 reverse stock split to regain Nasdaq compliance
data.description:
• HWH International (NASDAQ:HWH) announced a 1-for-5 reverse stock split of the company’s common stock to become effective at 9:00 a.m. ET on February 24, 2025.
  • HWH’s common stock is expected to begin trading on a split-adjusted basis when the markets open on February 24, 2025.
  • The reverse stock split is primarily intended to bring the company into compliance with the minimum bid price requirements for maintaining its listing on Nasdaq.
  • HWH shares fall over 22% premarket on Thursday.
  • Source: Press release

data.title: Codere Online Luxembourg reports Q4 results; initiates FY25 outlook and share buyback
data.description:
• Codere Online Luxembourg press release (NASDAQ:CDRO): Q4 net income excluding the non-cash variation in fair value of public warrants was €6.8M in 2024 versus a net loss of €4.0M in 2023.
  • Revenue of €50M (+6.6% Y/Y), while net gaming revenue was €52.6M in the period, 5% above Q4 2023.
  • Total cash position of €40.5M as of December 31, 2024.
  • Providing full year 2025 net gaming revenue outlook of €220M-230M and Adj. EBITDA outlook of €10M-15M.
  • The company’s board has authorized a share buyback plan of up to $5.0M, subject to shareholder approval.

data.title: Newegg launches NVIDIA RTX 5070 Ti GPUs and ABS Gaming PCs; shares rise
data.description:

  • The NVIDIA RTX 5070 Ti graphics cards as well as custom 5070 Ti powered PC systems will be available to purchase on Feb 20th.
  • NEGG shares rise 11.7% premarket on Thursday.

data.title: Intelligent Bio Solutions announces pricing of $2.6 million public offering; shares fall
data.description:
• Intelligent Bio Solutions (NASDAQ:INBS) on Thursday announced the pricing of an underwritten public offering of 1.30 million shares of common stock at $2.00 per share of common stock, for gross proceeds of approximately $2.6 million.
  • In addition, the company has granted the underwriter a 45-day option to purchase up to an additional 195,652 shares of its common stock at the public offering price less the underwriting discounts and commissions.
  • The offering is expected to close on or about February 21, 2025.
  • The company intends to use the net proceeds from this offering for working capital and general corporate purposes.
  • INBS -14.39% premarket to $2.26.
  • Source: Press Release
